_note: Orca Whirlpools is an Anchor program. Layouts verified against mainnet fixtures (2026-07). Discriminators: sha256('global:<snake_case_name>')[..8].

f8c69e91e17587c8.name: swap
f8c69e91e17587c8.note: Single-pool swap. 11 accounts. Mints resolved via tokenOwnerAccount→tokenAccountMints map.
f8c69e91e17587c8.mintResolution: tokenAccountMints[accounts[3]] = mintA; tokenAccountMints[accounts[5]] = mintB; a_to_b=true → input=A,output=B; false → input=B,output=A

2b04ed0b1ac91e62.name: swap_v2
2b04ed0b1ac91e62.note: Token-2022 compatible swap. 15+ accounts. Direct mints at accounts[5] and accounts[6].
2b04ed0b1ac91e62.mintResolution: accounts[5] = tokenMintA (direct); accounts[6] = tokenMintB (direct); a_to_b=true → input=A,output=B

c360ed6c44a2dbe6.name: two_hop_swap
c360ed6c44a2dbe6.note: Two-pool chained swap. Mint resolution via owner net flows (no direct mint accounts in v1).
c360ed6c44a2dbe6.mintResolution: ownerSpentMints[0] = inputMint; ownerGainedMints[0] = outputMint (net user flow)

ba8fd11dfe02c275.name: two_hop_swap_v2
ba8fd11dfe02c275.note: Token-2022 two-pool swap. Same args as two_hop_swap. Mint resolution via owner net flows.
ba8fd11dfe02c275.mintResolution: ownerSpentMints[0] = inputMint; ownerGainedMints[0] = outputMint (net user flow)
